zotero.abstractNote: We study the vulnerability of neural networks to small input perturbations, focusing on the first-order (gradient-based) adversarial vulnerability. We show that this vulnerability is closely related to the input dimension. Specifically, for a wide class of classifiers, the expected gradient norm is proportional to the square root of the input dimension. This result provides a theoretical explanation for why high-dimensional inputs are more susceptible to adversarial attacks. We also provide empirical evidence supporting our theoretical findings on several datasets and architectures.
